_comment: Project config for `relay` (the generic engine reads this — it hardcodes nothing about these columns/agents/skills). `pipeline` lists AI stages left-to-right; the runner works cards right-to-left. Per stage: `from` = column a ready card is pulled from, `done` = where to push when finished (a *:Review lane = human checkpoint, else auto-continues), `action` = ordered steps. Each step is {shell: <cmd>} or {claude: <prompt>}. Templates: {ref} {title} {branch} {stage} {from} {done} {relay} {url}. Regenerate the skeleton from the board with `relay layout`.

action.claude: You are the AI working Relay card {ref} at the SPEC stage. Read it: {relay} card {ref}. Design this feature — decide scope, keep it tight. If you genuinely need clarification, ask the human instead of guessing: `{relay} needs-input {ref} "<your question>"` then STOP. Otherwise write a concise design spec, save it to a temp file, and set it as the card's description: `{relay} describe {ref} @<that file>`. Then STOP. Do not touch git or other cards.

You are the AI working Relay card {ref} at the PLAN stage. Read it: `{relay} card {ref}` — its description is the approved spec. Author an implementation plan at the repo root as plan.md following this repo's /write-plan conventions (bite-sized TDD tasks with ACTUAL test + implementation code, no placeholders). Overwrite any existing plan.md. Then STOP. Do not touch git or other cards.

Run /exec-plan on the current branch to completion using the repo-root plan.md. This is authorized — proceed without asking for confirmation. Do not merge; exec-plan stops before the human-gated finish.

action.shell: git checkout main && git merge --no-ff {branch} -m 'Merge {branch} ({ref})' && (git ls-files --error-unmatch plan.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && git rm --cached plan.md && git commit -q -m 'chore: untrack throwaway plan.md' || true)
gh run watch $(gh run list --branch main --limit 1 --json databaseId --jq '.[0].databaseId') --exit-status
